meta.siteDescription: A colorful domino game for 1–8 players, featuring AI opponents and online multiplayer.
meta.rulesDescription: Complete Chromino rules including tile placement, turn flow, wild tiles, and variant modes.

home.subtitle: Puzzle Board Game · 1–8 Players · Local or Online
home.description: A colorful domino board game where players race to play all tiles from their hand to win
home.localSetupDesc: Add 1–8 seats. Seat 1 is you; others can be humans (pass-and-play) or AI.

game.shareCode: Share this code with friends. Total 1–8 players (including AI).
game.disbandConfirmMsg: The room will close and all players will be removed.
game.leaveConfirmMsg: You will leave the lobby. Other players are not affected.

playerPanel.leaveRoomMsg: Game progress will be lost. Confirm?

rules.sec1P1: Each player draws 8 tiles from the bag and takes turns placing tiles on the board.
rules.sec1P2: The first player to play all their tiles wins.
rules.sec2R1: Base Chrominos: 75 tiles, each a 1×3 rectangle with 3 colored cells from 5 colors
rules.sec2R2: Wild Chrominos (Chameleon): 5 tiles, normal colors on both ends, ◎ mark in the center that can be adjacent to any color
rules.sec2R3: Bag: 1 bag for storing and randomly drawing tiles
rules.sec2Info: Each Chromino is a 1×3 rectangle with 3 adjacent colored cells. Tiles can be rotated (horizontal or vertical) but not placed diagonally.
rules.sec3Info: Wild cell (◎): white with a ring mark, can be adjacent to any color — no color matching required.
rules.sec4Step1: Randomly pick 1 Wild Chromino, place it color-side up in the center of the table as the first tile.
rules.sec4Step2: Shuffle all remaining tiles (74 base + 4 wild) into the bag.
rules.sec4Step3: Each player blindly draws 8 tiles as their hand — only visible to themselves.
rules.sec4Step4: The youngest player goes first (or choose randomly), then play clockwise.
rules.sec5P1: When placing a tile, both of these must be true:
rules.sec5R1: Adjacent to at least 2 cells of tiles already on the board
rules.sec5R2: Every adjacent cell must be an exact color match (Wild ◎ excepted)
rules.sec5Info: Orientation & Rotation: Tiles can be rotated 0°, 90°, 180°, or 270°. Only horizontal or vertical placement is allowed — no diagonal.
rules.sec6R1: The center wild cell (◎) can be adjacent to any color — even two different colors at once
rules.sec6R2: The two end cells of a Wild Chromino are normal colors and must follow standard matching rules
rules.sec7CaseADesc: Play exactly 1 tile from your hand that satisfies placement rules. Turn ends.
rules.sec7CaseBDesc: Draw 1 tile from the bag. If it can be played, play it immediately; otherwise keep it. Turn ends.
rules.sec7Info: If the bag is empty and you cannot play, your turn is passed (Pass).
rules.sec7SR1: When you have only 1 tile left, you must reveal it so all players can see it
rules.sec7SR2: If your last tile is a Wild Chromino, you cannot play it — draw 1 more tile from the bag first
rules.sec8R1: The first player to play all their tiles wins — the game does not stop immediately
rules.sec8R1Strong: first player to play all their tiles
rules.sec8R2: All other players take one more turn
rules.sec8R3: If any other player also finishes on the final round, they win jointly
rules.sec9Var1Desc: Draw 1 when you cannot play; play immediately if possible, otherwise keep
rules.sec9Var2Desc: Keep drawing until you draw a tile you can play
rules.sec9Var3Desc: Draw up to 3 tiles; play if possible, otherwise keep the last drawn tile
rules.sec9Scoring1: The player who empties their hand scores 0; others score penalty points equal to their remaining tile count
rules.sec9Scoring2: After multiple games, the player with the fewest points wins
rules.sec9Var4Desc: Draw up to 3–5 tiles (set the limit before the game starts)
rules.sec9Other1Desc: All players reveal their hands — anyone can freely view them
rules.sec9Other2Desc: A tile only needs to be adjacent to at least 1 matching cell (good for teaching or younger players)
rules.qr7Val: Cannot play it — draw 1 more first
rules.svgPlacementAria: Placement rule examples: valid and invalid
rules.svgWildCellNote: ◎ can be adjacent to any color
